# UC-18.2.13 · NSX Manager Cluster Health

## Description

Monitors VMware NSX Manager Unified Appliance clusters where each node runs Manager (configuration store), Policy (intent-graph engine), and Search (indexed lookups) together. Corfu distributes the transaction log; Async Replicator Service (ARS) propagates intent versus realized states. Observe cluster-management-plane telemetry—Policy and Manager HTTPS, VIP ownership, Reverse Proxy on TCP 443 with the Layer-7 cluster certificate—distinct from cluster-control-plane duties such as Central Control Plane coordinating Local Control Plane agents on transport nodes. NSX 4.x assumes a three-node quorum needing two healthy members, emitting cluster_status UP/DEGRADED/DOWN alongside Corfu layout epochs, ARS lag, per-role divergence when Manager stays UP while Policy turns DEGRADED, GROUP_ID rebalance noise during repairs, SMALL/MEDIUM/LARGE cues, and five-second-class node-management-protocol heartbeats so gossip jitter is not mistaken for quorum failure—excluding GENEVE tunnels, Edge dataplanes, and Policy segments covered elsewhere.

## Value

Manager quorum loss stalls Policy pushes and CCP-to-LCP coordination while overlays still ride GENEVE—so outages masquerade as vague virtualization faults. Surfacing Corfu health, ARS lag, VIP churn, HTTPS Reverse Proxy posture, and Manager/Policy/Search splits yields a definitive management-plane storyline before Tier-1 dashboards confuse the blast radius.

## Implementation

Install Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt where vmware:nsxt:cluster_status and vmware:nsxt:syslog parse, add HTTPS collectors for Policy and Manager cluster routes, schedule saved searches with macros wrapping the supplied SPL, and wire tokens to dashboards plus PagerDuty routes for DEGRADED or quorum-loss classes. Quick win: enable vmware:nsxt:cluster_status from GET /policy/api/v1/cluster/status before finishing every per-node micro-poll.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) on **Search Heads**, **Heavy Forwarders** hosting HTTPS modular inputs, and collector hosts polling **NSX Manager Unified Appliance** clusters on **VMware NSX** **4.2** / **4.1** / **NSX-T** **3.2.x** aligned with Broadcom lifecycle guidance.
• Create least-privilege identities with roles covering **Policy** **cluster** endpoints, **Manager** **cluster** runtime routes, and **Aggregation Service** APIs—authenticate via **POST /api/session/create** cookies or **mutual TLS** rather than interactive admin credentials.
• Guarantee outbound **TCP 443** paths from collectors to each Manager FQDN, **cluster Virtual IP**, and **Reverse Proxy** front end that terminates the **Layer-7 cluster certificate** chain for REST consumers.
• Deploy **Splunk_TA_vmware** ([Splunkbase 1810](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) when **vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter** inventories must correlate **NSX Manager** VM identifiers with **ESXi** hosts during **GROUP_ID** rebalance drills.
• Budget indexes for HTTPS JSON polls plus syslog bursts—steady EPS stays modest yet upgrade waves amplify **Corfu** reconfiguration chatter and **Async Replicator Service (ARS)** catch-up logs.

Step 1 — Data sources
(1) **Policy** plane collectors should mirror **GET /policy/api/v1/cluster/status**, **GET /policy/api/v1/cluster/nodes**, and optional **GET /policy/api/v1/cluster/restore/status** into **vmware:nsxt:cluster_status** so **cluster_id**, **cluster_status**, **node_id**, **node_role**, **node_ip**, and **vip_owner** remain comparable minute-over-minute without screenshot gymnastics.
(2) Extend polls with **GET /api/v1/cluster/nodes/<node-id>/runtime/info** plus **GET /api/v1/cluster/nodes/<node-id>/services/<svc>/status** for discrete **Manager**, **Policy**, **Search**, **Central Control Plane (CCP)**, and **Reverse Proxy** modules listening on **HTTPS 443**—this exposes per-service divergence while aggregate labels stay UP.
(3) Capture **GET /api/v1/aggregation-service/cluster-statistics** payloads so Aggregation backlog proxies flatten into numeric **asr_lag_seconds** columns—stamp **source_system=aggregation** inside transforms when mixing with Policy feeds sharing one sourcetype.
(4) Forward Manager **/var/log/syslog** and sibling paths using **facility local6** conventions into **vmware:nsxt:syslog** while preserving RFC 5424 PRI headers so TA routing distinguishes cluster anecdotes from unrelated streams.
(5) Mirror authenticated mutations through **vmware:nsxt:audit** so deliberate membership edits tie to ServiceNow change tickets—critical when diagnosing quorum restores versus intrusion replay attempts.
(6) Optional **gRPC NSX Telemetry** may augment cluster-management-plane counters; FIELDALIAS overlays only after parity tests versus HTTPS collectors so engineers do not chase exporter skew.
(7) Scripted **Corfu transaction log** snapshots can seed **corfu_layout_state** epochs—treat these as supplemental to Policy truth during Sev-1 command.

**Quick win:** Ship **vmware:nsxt:cluster_status** ingestion for **GET /policy/api/v1/cluster/status** before deeper per-service scrapes—teams see **cluster_status** and **VIP** posture within one sprint.

Step 2 — Field extractions
Normalize FIELDALIAS chains for **cluster_id**, **cluster_status**, **node_id**, **node_role**, **node_status**, **node_ip**, **manager_status**, **policy_status**, **search_status**, **ccp_status**, **corfu_layout_state**, **asr_lag_seconds**, **etcd_status**, **vip_owner**, **version**, **uptime_seconds**, and appliance sizing enums **SMALL**, **MEDIUM**, **LARGE**—camelCase REST keys must reconcile with syslog tokens without duplicate Inventory entities.

Step 3 — Initial validation
Cross-check Splunk aggregates against NSX CLI **get cluster status** outputs plus **get cluster status verbose** Corfu narratives—CLI remains authoritative when HTTPS collectors disagree about membership rosters or VIP ownership.
Validate against vSphere Client widgets enumerating Manager VMs—power-state anomalies or VMware Tools faults explain syslog gaps absent Corfu loss.
Compare Splunk timelines with vRealize Network Insight Manager-cluster cards—Splunk GREEN versus VRNI RED typically reveals stale collectors or RBAC drift rather than invisible fabrics.
Exercise HTTPS probes through the Reverse Proxy with the same trust anchors collectors use—TLS handshake failures mimic DOWN clusters though data planes stay healthy.

Step 4 — SPL building blocks
VIP flap: chain **streamstats** on **vip_owner** per **cluster_id** to count leadership moves per minute—more than one unplanned hop triggers escalation absent matching ServiceNow maintenance metadata.
Corfu drift: compare sequential **corfu_layout_state** integers—monotonic climbs align with documented GROUP_ID repairs whereas oscillation hints thrash needing VMware support bundles.
ARS lag growth: combine rising **asr_lag_seconds** slopes with sustained DEGRADED labels—pair thresholds near sixty to one hundred twenty seconds with two successive breaches before paging.
Per-service splits: alert when **manager_status** stays UP yet **policy_status** or **search_status** reads DEGRADED/DOWN—the Policy intent-graph engine may choke while configuration APIs respond.
Quorum loss: correlate DOWN or LOST_QUORUM strings with counts of healthy nodes below two-of-three proofs—overlay Policy restore feeds whenever administrators invoke documented quorum-restoration workflows after snapshot recoveries.

Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns (deepest section)
Bind **Splunk Add-on for VMware** VM facts to node identifiers so responders know which ESXi host shelters a failing Manager appliance before mobilizing hardware swaps.
Cross-validate Splunk panels with Splunk Add-on for vRealize Network Insight feeds when VRNI licenses exist—Management plane truth should match before opening sev bridges.
Layer CMDB ownership attributes on **cluster_id** rows so escalation lists map to accountable network platform teams instead of generic infrastructure bridges.
Integrate ServiceNow CHG lookups for upgrades such as NSX 4.x rolling progressions into NSX 4.2.x so ARS lag noise during intentionally quiesced nodes does not duplicate operational incidents.
Reference **MITRE ATT&CK T1499.004** when security operations must rule out adversarial service-stop behaviors that resemble cluster regressions—pair with hypervisor alarms to avoid false criminal attributions.
Correlate vCenter alarms referencing DRS migrations, datastore latency, or HA restarts against Manager syslog noise—those activities reset Reverse Proxy sessions without implying Corfu quorum collapse.
Where licensed, map Splunk ITSI services so **NSX Manager cluster** KPI children sit beneath higher-level fabric parents covering NSX Edge clusters and transport zones—this ordering keeps management-plane starvation visible before dataplane KPIs redden and differentiates VMware NSX constructs from UC-18.3.3 generic SDN controllers that watch Akka lifecycles unrelated to Unified Appliance trio roles.

Step 6 — Operational runbook
Declare Sev-1 quorum loss when **cluster_status** hits DOWN outside approved change windows or when two of three Unified Appliance roles fail concurrently—freeze Policy automation, capture Proxy diagnostics, attach Splunk extracts, target acknowledgment MTTR under fifteen minutes and full service restoration MTTR under one hundred twenty minutes when VMware restore kits apply.
Follow Manager cluster restore workflows referencing **set cluster restore** plus backup validation checklists—archive Splunk captures of **corfu_layout_state** and **asr_lag_seconds** before and after restores for auditors.
Treat rolling upgrades as expected DEGRADED intervals—suppress alerts using ServiceNow stamps plus proof that two nodes remain healthy while the third reboots—median MTBF between repeat regressions should exceed ninety days once upgrades stabilize.
Dashboard wiring should bind tokens for cluster summary tiles, Manager/Policy/Search/CCP matrices, VIP ownership timelines, Corfu layout-version columns, and ARS lag heatmaps—tokens keep drills scoped to management-plane observability rather than Edge dataplane dashboards documented elsewhere.

Governance cadence & instrumentation hygiene
Rotate HTTPS collectors across staggered offsets per Manager cluster so simultaneous polls never stampede Reverse Proxy workers during nightly backups yet SOC detection budgets still beat fifteen-minute expectations referenced inside VMware operational guides. Maintain indextime lag analytics so indexer squads review whenever Splunk ingestion skew stretches wider than ninety seconds—otherwise ARS lag math mislabels ingestion backlog as replication faults. Tie Splunk alert recipients to quarterly tabletop drills centered on Unified Appliance failures so escalation bridges rehearse Policy SMEs alongside virtualization SMEs authorized for Policy infrastructure namespaces rather than bouncing incidents solely toward unrelated SDN controller owners described inside UC-18.3.3 narratives.

Heartbeat and protocol context
Document the default node-management-protocol heartbeat near five-second cadence inside your runbooks so operators interpret brief DEGRADED blips inside that window as normal gossip stabilization rather than instant Corfu catastrophe; lengthened heartbeats during maintenance correlate with deliberate service quiescence, not stealth attacks, when ServiceNow approvals exist.

Privacy & credential considerations
Collector identities often possess Enterprise Admin scopes—guard secrets inside PKCS#12 keystores encrypted per Splunk credential services, rotate client certificates before VMware NSX appliance certificate rotations every ninety days when your PKI mandates align, and document which jump hosts may run CLI validation so evidence packets stay reproducible during audits.

Documentation anchors
Archive nightly CSV exports beside Policy cluster JSON snapshots whenever Sev-1 bridges close—auditors expect timestamp parity proving Splunk mirrored authoritative APIs rather than reconstructed guesses.

## SPL

```spl
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:cluster_status" OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(cluster.?status|corfu|vip|ccp|reverse.?proxy|quorum|manager.?service|policy.?service|search.?service|ars|async.?replicator|layout.?epoch)")))
earliest=-4h@m
| eval cluster_id=trim(coalesce(cluster_id,nsx_cluster_uuid,cluster_uuid,manager_cluster_id,""))
| eval cluster_status=upper(trim(coalesce(cluster_status,overall_cluster_health,aggregate_status,"")))
| eval node_id=trim(coalesce(node_id,node_uuid,member_id,entity_id,""))
| eval node_role=upper(trim(coalesce(node_role,appliance_role,role,"")))
| eval manager_status=upper(trim(coalesce(manager_status,mgr_svc_status,config_store_status,"")))
| eval policy_status=upper(trim(coalesce(policy_status,policy_plane_status,intent_status,"")))
| eval search_status=upper(trim(coalesce(search_status,search_plane_status,index_svc_status,"")))
| eval corfu_layout_state=upper(trim(coalesce(corfu_layout_state,corfu_state,corfu_layout_epoch,"")))
| eval asr_lag_seconds=tonumber(coalesce(asr_lag_seconds,ars_lag_seconds,replication_lag_sec,async_rep_lag,"0"))
| eval vip_owner=trim(coalesce(vip_owner,vip_holder,active_vip_node,vip_leader,""))
| eval ccp_status=upper(trim(coalesce(ccp_status,ccp_health,"")))
| eval quorum_hint=case(match(cluster_status,"^(DOWN|LOST_QUORUM|NO_QUORUM)$"),"cluster_quorum_bad", cluster_status="DEGRADED","cluster_degraded", null())
| sort 0 cluster_id node_id _time
| streamstats window=3 global=f latest(vip_owner) as vip_prev by cluster_id
| eval vip_flap=if(isnotnull(vip_prev) AND vip_prev!=vip_owner AND len(vip_owner)>0,1,0)
| eval svc_split=case(manager_status="UP" AND match(policy_status,"^(DEGRADED|DOWN)$"),"mgr_ok_policy_bad", manager_status="UP" AND policy_status="UP" AND match(search_status,"^(DEGRADED|DOWN)$"),"search_isolated", policy_status="UP" AND match(manager_status,"^(DEGRADED|DOWN)$"),"mgr_bad_policy_ok", null())
| eval ars_hot=if(asr_lag_seconds>120 OR (asr_lag_seconds>60 AND cluster_status!="UP"),1,0)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
    latest(cluster_status) as cs,
    latest(manager_status) as ms,
    latest(policy_status) as ps,
    latest(search_status) as ss,
    latest(corfu_layout_state) as corfu_st,
    max(asr_lag_seconds) as asr_pk,
    latest(vip_owner) as vip_now,
    sum(vip_flap) as vip_moves,
    values(svc_split) as svc_splits,
    values(quorum_hint) as q_hints,
    latest(ccp_status) as ccp_st,
    dc(node_id) as nodes_seen,
    latest(node_role) as role_hint
  by cluster_id _time
| where cs IN ("DOWN","DEGRADED","UNKNOWN","LOST_QUORUM","NO_QUORUM") OR asr_pk>90 OR vip_moves>0 OR mvcount(svc_splits)>0 OR mvcount(q_hints)>0 OR ars_hot=1
| sort -last_seen
| table last_seen cluster_id cs ms ps ss corfu_st asr_pk vip_now vip_moves svc_splits q_hints ccp_st nodes_seen role_hint
```

## Visualization

(1) Single-value / gauge cluster_status per cluster_id with drilldown to Policy UI links. (2) Matrix panel of Manager vs Policy vs Search vs CCP status tiles keyed by node_id. (3) Timeline chart of vip_owner changes with VIP flap annotations from streamstats logic. (4) Column or sparkline chart plotting corfu_layout_state versions across _time. (5) Heatmap or choropleth-style matrix (cluster_id × five-minute buckets) coloring max(asr_lag_seconds). (6) Optional supplement: Combined alert table listing svc_splits and quorum_hint classes.
